---
name: Propose New Area
about: Suggest a new top-level domain or subdomain for OpenScientist
title: "[New Area] <domain-name>"
labels: "area: proposed"
assignees: HHHHHejia
---

## Area Name

<!-- What would the folder be called? e.g. `earth-science`, `materials-science` -->

## Type

- [ ] New top-level domain (new folder directly under `skills/`)
- [ ] New subdomain (subfolder within an existing domain — no approval needed, just include in your PR)

## Scope

<!-- What kinds of skills would live here? What research questions would they address?
     Be specific enough to distinguish this area from existing domains. -->

## Why it belongs in OpenScientist

<!-- Why is this area valuable for AI-assisted scientific reasoning?
     What frontier problems could AI agents tackle with these skills? -->

## Overlap check

<!-- Does this overlap with any existing domain? If so, why should it be separate? -->

## Proposed reviewer

<!-- Who will review PRs for this domain? Provide their GitHub handle and affiliation.
     A new top-level domain requires a committed reviewer before it can be accepted. -->

- Name:
- GitHub:
- Affiliation:

*Once approved, the core team will add the label `area: accepted` and you can proceed with a PR.*
